description_long: This rifle features a robust, dark metal body with a prominent circular drum magazine mounted on top, characteristic of early 20th-century light machine guns. The stock is made of polished wood with visible grain and a metal buttplate, providing a classic and sturdy appearance. The barrel is thick and extends forward, ending in a simple muzzle with no visible attachments. The receiver area includes various mechanical components, such as a charging handle and trigger assembly, with some exposed springs and levers. The drum magazine is large and ribbed, suggesting high ammunition capacity, and the rifle is equipped with basic iron sights. The overall construction combines metal and wood, giving it a utilitarian yet historical look suitable for urban or historical settings.
description: A vintage-style rifle with a large top-mounted drum magazine, dark metal body, and polished wooden stock, featuring iron sights and exposed mechanical parts.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular drum magazine on top, the barrel's end, and the alignment of the iron sights.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the rifle, the wooden stock, the large drum magazine, and the detailed receiver area.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the wooden stock, the back of the drum magazine, and the iron sight alignment.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the other side of the receiver, the drum magazine, and the wooden stock.
